TextENGNew: I was lounging in the living room, cooling off, when I heard a voice calling me from the front entrance.
It was Kyouko, our housekeeper. Someone had come to visit.
changeReason: Old translation calls Kyouko a 'nanny'; new correctly calls her 'housekeeper' matching JPN お手伝い (household helper).

TextENGNew: I couldn't quite catch the name, but whoever it was seemed to be here for me.
changeReason: 'customer' implies a business relationship; new translation correctly reflects JPN お客さん as a visitor/guest without commercial connotation.

TextENGNew: Our house sits some distance from the gate, which makes it hard to notice visitors.@k We'd been planning to install an intercom—partly for security—sometime soon.
changeReason: Old fabricates content about doorbells and yelling; JPN describes distance from gate making visitors hard to notice and plans to install an intercom for security.

TextENGNew: ``Ah, yes, thank you.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2037. Who is it?``
Poking my head into the hallway as I said that, I spotted Rika-chan standing in the entryway beside Kyouko.
changeReason: Old says Mion 'walked into the hallway' but JPN 顔だけ出す means she only peeked her face out; also 'Kyoko' corrected to 'Kyouko' matching the name used elsewhere.

TextENGNew: Normally, guests would be greeted at the gate, but Kyouko must have ushered her inside so she wouldn't have to wait in the blazing sun.

``Oh, there you are.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2039. Could you let Granny know Rika-chama is here?@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2040. She's probably on the veranda.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2041. I'll go pull the watermelon up from the well.``
Granny had received a whole bunch of gorgeous watermelons as a gift, so she decided to share them around.
I had just called the Furude household a short while ago, so Rika-chan must have come to pick one up.
...Did she come by bicycle?
Was the front basket on Rika-chan's bicycle even big enough for a watermelon?
changeReason: New specifies the front basket (前かご) and whether a watermelon would fit, matching JPN precisely; old was vague about a 'big enough basket'.

TextENGNew: Fretting over that needless worry, I hauled up the watermelon that had been hanging in the well out back.
Having been chilled in the cold well water, the watermelon was beaded with cool droplets and looked absolutely delicious.
For the three-person Furude household, it would last at least two evenings.
``...Mii?@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2039. I'm supposed to take it home?@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2040. I was sure we'd be...``
changeReason: 'Meep' changed to 'Mii' which directly transliterates JPN みー; also better captures the nuance of 'I was sure we'd be...' from JPN てっきり.

TextENGNew: ``Huh?@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2043. Didn't your father tell you?!``
changeReason: 'dad' changed to 'father'; JPN おじさん here refers to Rika's father the priest, and 'father' better fits the formal register.

TextENGNew: I had told the priest who answered the phone that we had watermelon and to come pick one up, or so I thought...
Whether my phrasing was unclear or the message got garbled, it seems Rika-chan was under the impression she'd been invited over@k to eat watermelon here, rather than take one home.
The small basket on Rika-chan's bicycle couldn't possibly hold a watermelon this size,@k and riding one-handed while balancing it would be downright dangerous.
``Mion, be a dear and carry it to Rika-chama's house for her.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2044. She can't ride her bicycle holding a watermelon.``
``...Mii.@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2042. I can carry a watermelon, you know.``
changeReason: 'Meep' changed to 'Mii' matching JPN みー, Rika's characteristic vocalization.

TextENGNew: ``Too dangerous, too dangerous!@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2046. If anything happened to Rika-chama, I wouldn't know how to face Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2047. Mion will carry it for you, just leave it to her.``
``All right, all right, leave it to me.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2045. I'll pop in and say hello to the priest while I'm at it.``
Whether riding or walking her bicycle, Rika-chan's hands would be occupied either way.
So I was appointed watermelon carrier.
The sky was tinged with a faint amber glow.
changeReason: 'auburn' changed to 'amber'; JPN 茜色 (akane-iro) means madder-red, a different hue from either, but changes the reader's visual impression.

TextENGNew: ...A wonderfully cool breeze was blowing, and the sandals on my bare feet felt lovely.
The plastic grocery bag I carried held the watermelon, along with several small bags of konpeitou sugar candy—a little bonus@k for Rika-chan, since she'd come all the way to the Sonozaki main house.
changeReason: 'rock candy' changed to 'konpeitou sugar candy'; JPN 金平糖 is konpeitou, a specific Japanese confection distinct from generic rock candy.

TextENGNew: Apparently, if her mother found them she'd confiscate them, so we decided to eat them on the spot. We opened the bags and savored the simple sweetness of the konpeitou...
It wasn't just for Rika-chan, actually—whenever a neighborhood child came by on an errand,@k Granny's rule was to give them a little treat as a reward.
In fact, that sort of custom is fairly common here in rural Hinamizawa.
It even leads to funny stories about siblings racing each other to deliver the circular notice to the next house before their parents have even read it.
...This konpeitou was one such treat.
changeReason: 'rock candy' changed to 'konpeitou', correctly identifying the specific Japanese confection 金平糖.

TextENGNew: Though normally, the reward would be much more modest.
A single piece of hard candy at most.
...So this was clearly Granny giving Rika-chama special treatment.
``...I feel bad getting so much more than everyone else.``
changeReason: New captures Rika's concern about receiving more than others (JPN 他の人に悪い気がします = feel bad toward others), which the old translation omitted.

TextENGNew: ``Oh, don't worry about it.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2047. It's Granny's way of showing affection.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2048. Rather than making a fuss about declining, I think it's better to just be a kid about it—say thank you and give her a big smile.``
``...Does Mii get lots of treats?``
changeReason: 'you' changed to 'Mii', preserving Rika's distinctive nickname for Mion (JPN 魅ぃ).

TextENGNew: ``Hmm, I used to!@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2050. But at my age, they've stopped giving me any.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2051. If they did now, I'd probably feel like I was being treated like a little kid, ha!``
``...Mii.@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2046. So I'm being treated like a child, then.``
changeReason: 'Meep' changed to 'Mii' matching JPN みー.

TextENGNew: ``Ahahahahaha, that's perfectly fine!@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2053. Enjoy it while you can.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2054. Once you're my age, you won't get any even if you wanted them.``
We'd been chatting about nothing in particular, so I was surprised to notice Rika-chan's expression turning cloudy.
``What's wrong?``
``...Whenever I go shopping, I always get candy as a treat.@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2048. ...But Satoko doesn't, even when she comes with me.``
changeReason: 'run errands' changed to 'go shopping' matching JPN お買い物; also adds frequency aspect 'always' from JPN よく.

TextENGNew: I could read many things into why Rika-chan would say that to me... and there was no end to it.
changeReason: Old oversimplifies to 'so many reasons why'; new captures the full JPN meaning of endlessly deep-reading Rika's intent (深読みしたらきりがない).

TextENGNew: It's no secret to anyone in Hinamizawa that the Houjou family—not just Satoko—is treated coldly by the entire village.
...And the ones who fanned those flames were none other than the Sonozaki main house.
changeReason: 'Sonozaki family' changed to 'Sonozaki main house' matching JPN 園崎本家, which specifically refers to the main branch—an important distinction in the clan structure.

TextENGNew: Moreover, the two dam supporters from the Houjou family had already died in an accident.
And yet the hostility toward the Houjou family continued unabated,@k extending even to the children, Satoko and Satoshi,@k ...and to the aunt and uncle who had taken them in.
It's true that the bitter feud between the Houjou and Sonozaki families at the dam project meeting was what started the conflict.
...But that was the Houjou couple. It should have nothing to do with the children or their aunt and uncle.
...Yet somehow, the label 'village traitors equals Houjou family' took on a life of its own, creating an atmosphere that continues to shun anyone bearing the Houjou name...
The aunt and uncle were never dam supporters.
So with time, the attacks against them should have ceased.
...But it turned out the aunt and uncle were difficult people who kept getting into trouble with the neighbors.
Ignoring the garbage disposal rules, forgetting their turn on the rotation roster, spraying pesticide on their garden trees without regard for the neighbors' laundry...
...They apparently quarreled frequently with the community council and the neighbors. Even without the dam conflict, they would have been disliked.
...And so, the cold treatment of the Houjou family persisted.
But even so, none of it should have anything to do with Satoko and Satoshi...
At school, I've often seen the strained expressions on Satoko's and Satoshi's faces.
changeReason: 'depressed' changed to 'strained expressions'; JPN 辛そうな顔 means pained/strained faces, an observable description rather than a clinical diagnosis.

TextENGNew: ...I try to keep the Houjou-Sonozaki conflict out of school, at the very least... but I have no way of knowing how Satoko and the others truly feel about that.
From their perspective, I'm just another member of the family that makes their lives miserable...
``...I don't want any of that in the school, at least.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2058. That's why I try to be their friend while we're there.``
``...How can that same spirit be extended beyond the school walls...?``
...That would be terribly difficult.
The Houjou family made enemies of the Three Families.
They were branded as traitors, and that stigma remains to this day.
Even if one person stood up and said, 'The dam war is over, let's all get along,' it would be like pouring water on a hot stone—utterly futile.
...If anything, it would just make that person a target as well.
changeReason: 'put them in the same position' changed to 'make that person a target'; JPN 後ろ指をさされる means being gossiped about behind one's back.

TextENGNew: ...I feel for Satoko and the others, but@k their only option is to endure patiently until the animosity cools on its own.
The most I can do is try to be a small refuge for them until that day comes.
``...Then how long must Satoko endure...?``
changeReason: 'wait' changed to 'endure' matching JPN 耐え忍べば (to endure patiently), a much heavier word than simply waiting.

TextENGNew: ``...I don't know.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2060. The dam conflict lasted years.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2061. Since the stigma was branded back then, it might take just as long to wash it away.``

``...Does that mean@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2052. Satoko and the others won't be forgiven until they're grown?@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2053. That Satoko has to spend her entire childhood under those cold stares?``
``That would be... unbearably cruel...``
changeReason: 'pretty miserable' significantly understates JPN あまりにも辛い (unbearably painful/cruel); new translation captures the emotional weight.

TextENGNew: ``...Mii.@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2055. We're Satoko's and Satoshi's friends, aren't we?``
``Of course.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2064. To me, they're my friends.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2065. We're all in this together.``
``...Then isn't there something we can do to save our friends...?``
changeReason: 'help' changed to 'save' matching JPN 救う which means to save/rescue, carrying much stronger urgency than merely helping.

TextENGNew: ``...What could we possibly do...?``
Even if the Sonozaki main house declared that the dam war was over and everyone should be kind to the Houjou family,@k that alone wouldn't solve anything.
In the first place, Granny still despises the Houjou family so fiercely that even uttering their name in her presence is taboo.
This isn't a matter of one person forgiving another.
...It's a matter of the village as a community—where countless human connections are tangled in complex ways.
...Making an enemy of that community is a terrifying thing.
Everyone knows that, which is why no one neglects their neighborly relations.
...Anyone who deliberately makes enemies of it will face the consequences.
...That is the unwritten law of Japanese community life.
And the Sonozaki family is woven into that very law.
Once the hostility toward the Houjou family took root, not even the Sonozaki family could easily sever it.
``...Are you saying that even a problem within Hinamizawa... cannot be solved by Hinamizawa's own people?``
``.........Sadly... that may be the case.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2068. If there were someone who could save Satoko,``
``...it would have to be someone unburdened by the village's entanglements.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2070. ...But no such person exists here.``
``...Are you saying someone from outside the village... has to come to her rescue...?``
Could it really happen—a knight in shining armor arriving from beyond the village, as Rika-chan suggested, to send a fresh wind through this stale air that has festered for hundreds of years?
It couldn't.
It was just wishful thinking, same as always.
...Time would eventually solve it.
Until then, the least I could do was be their ally.
Nothing more than that came to mind.
Rika-chan and I crunched on our konpeitou, able to do nothing more than pray that someday, everyone would forgive Satoko and her family...
Rika-chan gently broke the bitter silence.
changeReason: 'heavy silence' changed to 'bitter silence' matching JPN 苦々しい which specifically means bitter/unpleasant.
'I know' changed to 'Surely' better matching JPN きっと (surely/certainly); 'I know' implies certain knowledge while 'Surely' conveys hopeful conviction.

TextENGNew: ``...Surely... someone unbound by the village's ties will come here from outside.``

``That's right.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2074. Now that the dam war's over, it'd be great if people moved in from outside.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2075. Wouldn't it be fun if a kid around our age transferred in?``

``...He will come.@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2062. I know it.@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2063. And together with him, we will shatter this village's wicked customs.@k@vS20/05/VTK2_rika2064. ...That will be the first key to the three locks sealing our future.``
``...Huh?@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2077. What's that?@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2078. Are you quoting a novel or something?``
Rika-chan said cryptic things like that from time to time.
When she did, she seemed so mature... I almost doubted she was really younger than me.
The irrational persecution of the Houjou family...
...Would someone truly appear—someone free of the village's chains—to break it all apart...?
